senses.gloss: a beast of prey; a voracious animal

examples.text: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ وَالدَّمُ وَلَحْمُ الْخِنزِيرِ وَمَا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ بِهِ وَالْمُنْخَنِقَةُ وَالْمَوْقُوذَةُ وَالْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ وَالنَّطِيحَةُ وَمَا أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ إِلَّا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ وَمَا ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُبِ وَأَن تَسْتَقْسِمُوا بِالْأَزْلَامِ

etymology.text: From the root س ب ع (s b ʕ); compare سَبْعَة (sabʕa, “seven”).

body.text: The place to which mankind shall be congregated

(TA.) Hence the trad., (K, TA,) which relates that while a pastor was among his sheep, or goats, the wolf rushed upon him, and took from them a sheep, or goat, and the pastor pursued him until he rescued it from him; whereupon the wolf looked aside towards him, and said to him, (TA,)
(K, TA:) thus expl. by I Aar, and mentioned by Sgh and the author of the L: (TA:) but to this is contradictory, or repugnant,

,]) the saying of the wolf, (K, TA,) after the words mentioned above, (TA,) “ the day when it shall have no pastor but me; ” for the wolf will not be a pastor on the day of resurrection: or the meaning is,
who shall be for it on the occasion of trials, when it shall be left to itself, without pastor, a spoil to the animals of prey:
the animal of prey being thus made to be a pastor to it: (K, TA:) this is in the way of a trope: and accord. to this explanation, it may be [↓
a festival of their's in the Time of Ignorance, on which they were diverted from everything by their sport:
(AO, K, TA:) and accord. to one relation [of the trad.] it is with damm to the
, (Mgh, Msb,) of which the former is a contraction, (Msb,)
A seventh part; one of seven parts;
; (S, Msb, K;) the last not heard by Sh on any authority beside that of AZ: (TA:) pl. of the first (Msb) and second (Mgh, Msb)
] in which one reads: said to be postclassical. (Mgh.)       -A2-  See also
their coming to the water on the seventh day
counting the day of the next preceding watering as the first
]; (K;) or [in other words, which have virtually the same meaning,]
their remaining in their places of pasturing five complete days, and coming to the water on the sixth day, not reckoning the day of the
His camels came to the water

, (Sgh, Msb, K,) a dial. var., (Sgh, Msb,) and the form in common use with the vulgar, (Msb,) adopted also by several readers of the Kur in v. 4, (Msb, TA,) and often occurring in the poems of the Arabs, (TA,) and ↓
, (Sgh, K,) a form adopted by two readers of the Kur in the place above mentioned, and perhaps a dial. var., (Sgh, TA,) The
being sometimes applied to the latter, as, for instance, in the K, voce
; but generally to the former:] or
canine tooth, with which it makes hostile attacks, and seizes its prey;
[to which it is particularly applied by most of the Arabs in the present day,]
such as the wolf and the lynx and the leopard,
and the like of these, that has a fang, and attacks men and beasts and makes them its prey:

(TA:) the fox, however, is not thus called, though having a fang, (Msb, TA,) because he does not attack with it nor take prey, (Msb,) or because he does not attack small beasts, nor seize with his fang any animal; (TA;) and in like manner the hyena (Msb, TA) is not reckoned among the hostile animals thus called, wherefore the Sunneh allows that its flesh may be eaten, and requires that a compensation be made for it [by the sacrifice of a ram] if it be smitten [and killed] in the sacred territory or by a person in the state of ihrám: but
as to the jackal, it is a noxious
, and its flesh is unlawful, because it is of the same kind as wolves, except that it is smaller in size and weaker in body: thus says Az: but some others say that the
]: and it is said in the Mufradát to be thus called because of the perfectness of its strength; for
[seven] is one of the perfect numbers: (TA:) the pl. is
, (Sb, S, Msb, K,) i. e., of
, which has no other pl.; (Sb, Msb;)
is also a pl., (Sgh, Msb, K,) but this is pl. of pauc. of ↓
, (Sgh, Msb,) which, not being a contraction [of
, but a dial. var. thereof], has also for its pls. [of mult.]

body.text_bw: maA huwa A_il~aA sabuEN mina Als~ibaAEi
body.text_ar: مَا هُوَ اـِلَّا سَبُعٌ مِنَ السِّبَاعِ

body.text: He is none other than one of the animals of prey
is also the name of (assumed tropical:)
Centaurus, containing nineteen stars in the figure.
